title: carya ovalis var. holmesiana
intro: Carya ovalis var. holmesiana, also known as the swamp hickory or Holmes' hickory, is a variety of deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive oval-shaped leaves and edible nuts. The bark and nuts of the Carya ovalis var. holmesiana have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Carya ovalis var. holmesiana typically involve drying and grinding the bark and nuts, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
